title: Build a Bloch Sphere Visualizer
description: Create a small interactive tool that shows how single-qubit states move on the Bloch sphere under common gates.
whyItMatters: Bloch-sphere intuition turns abstract amplitudes into something you can reason about visually. It is one of the fastest ways to internalise single-qubit behaviour.

steps.title: Represent a qubit state
steps.description: Choose a state representation that lets you convert amplitudes into Bloch-sphere coordinates.
steps.tips: Start with pure states before adding mixed-state support.

steps.title: Implement basic gates
steps.description: Add at least X, Y, Z, H, and rotation gates so the viewer can show meaningful movement.
steps.tips: A few trusted gate examples are better than many buggy ones.

steps.title: Render the sphere and state vector
steps.description: Show the current qubit state on a sphere with clear axes and labels.
steps.tips: The educational value comes from clarity, not graphics complexity.

steps.title: Add sequence playback
steps.description: Let the user apply a short sequence of gates and watch the state evolve.
steps.tips: This is where phase and rotation intuition start to click.

steps.title: Explain one non-obvious result
steps.description: Write a note on why two different gate sequences can reach the same or different final states.
steps.tips: Use the tool to teach, not only to display.

deliverables: Short note explaining the state representation

rubric.description: Gate operations and displayed state positions are mathematically consistent.
A learner can understand what they are seeing without guessing.
The output teaches something beyond static textbook diagrams.
